Our Beliefs

Our Philosophy

What we believe about peptides, research, transparency, and why we built this shelf the way we did.

We believe every person deserves access to affordable, safe, and discreet peptides — without compromising on purity, without the runaround, and without the inflated prices. That's not a marketing line. It's the reason this shelf exists.

We built the shelf we wished existed.

The peptide space has a trust problem. Sourcing claims are vague. Purity numbers are inflated. And customers are expected to just take it on faith. We started Your Peptide Shelf because we were tired of it.

"Every peptide on this shelf is verified, sourced with integrity, and backed by research. We don't cut corners on purity, we don't hide behind vague claims, and we don't sell compounds we wouldn't use ourselves."

— Your Peptide Shelf

Purity Non-Negotiable

99.5%+ is a hard floor. Every batch independently tested in Europe. No rounding. No approximating.

Transparency Over Hype

Real published research on every product page. We explain what the science says and what it doesn't.

Evidence-Based Always

We only carry compounds with a substantive research profile backed by peer-reviewed journals and clinical trials.

Researcher First

Every decision is made for the person on the other end of this transaction. Not the algorithm, not the margin.

shopping_bag

Education

Knowledge Center

All articles are for educational purposes only. Research compounds are intended solely for laboratory use.

Fundamentals

What Are Peptides?

Peptides are short chains of amino acids linked together by peptide bonds. While proteins contain hundreds of amino acids, peptides generally contain fewer than 50. This smaller size allows them to interact with highly specific biological receptors and signalling pathways.

Synthetic peptides are engineered versions designed to mimic, amplify, or modify natural biological processes — often with enhanced stability and receptor selectivity compared to their natural counterparts.

Synthesis & Purity

How Are Research Peptides Made?

Modern research peptides are produced using Solid-Phase Peptide Synthesis (SPPS). Amino acids are sequentially added to a growing chain anchored to a solid resin. After synthesis, peptides undergo purification via HPLC to remove impurities, then freeze-dried (lyophilized) into a stable powder. Purity is confirmed using HPLC and Mass Spectrometry — all products on this shelf are verified at 99.5%+ purity.

Storage

How to Store Peptides Correctly

Lyophilized Powder

Store at -20C in a sealed container. Stable for 12-24 months. Avoid freeze-thaw cycles and moisture.

Reconstituted Solution

Refrigerate at 2-8C and use within 28 days. Label each vial with the reconstitution date.

Reconstitution

Peptide Reconstitution Guide

1

Calculate volume

Divide mg by desired mg/mL. E.g. 10mg at 5mg/mL = add 2mL BAC water.

2

Inject gently down the vial wall

Never blast directly onto the powder — prevents foaming and denaturation.

3

Swirl gently — never shake

Shaking causes foaming which can damage the peptide. Swirl slowly until clear.

4

Label and refrigerate immediately

Mark date, peptide name, and concentration. Store at 2-8C. Use within 28 days.

Research Context

What Does "For Research Purposes Only" Mean?

Research peptides have not completed the full regulatory approval process for pharmaceutical use in humans. They are sold legally for in vitro research, laboratory investigation, and scientific study — not for human or animal administration.

Disclaimer

All products sold by Your Peptide Shelf are intended strictly for laboratory and scientific research. Customers are responsible for compliance with all applicable laws in their jurisdiction.

Purity Standards

What Does 99.5%+ Purity Actually Mean?

Purity refers to the percentage of the desired peptide sequence in the final product. At 99.5%+, less than 0.5% of material consists of truncated sequences, by-products, or residual solvents. This is measured by reverse-phase HPLC and confirmed by mass spectrometry. Our European testing partners provide both HPLC and MS data for every batch.

All articles are for educational purposes only. Products are for research use only.

Shipping FAQ

Do you ship outside Canada?

Yes — worldwide at $40 CAD flat rate. International customers are responsible for customs and import regulations in their country.

How long does delivery take?

Canadian orders typically arrive within 3–8 business days from dispatch. Orders placed before 3 PM EDT ship same day.

How do I track my order?

A tracking number is generated and emailed to you once dispatched. Contact yourpeptideshelf@gmail.com if you haven't received tracking within 5 business days.

Will the packaging identify Your Peptide Shelf?

No. All orders ship in plain, unmarked packaging. The return address will not identify our business by name.

What payment methods do you accept?

USDC on Solana and Interac E-Transfer for Canadian customers. All prices in CAD.

/ BPC-157 / TB-500 — Wolverine Stack

Systemic Repair
99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 10 MG Blend

Peptide Blend

BPC-157 / TB-500

The Wolverine Stack

$50.00 / 10 mg vial

BPC-157 and TB-500 are two of the most researched regenerative peptides available. Combined into a single 10 mg blend, they work through complementary pathways — BPC-157 targeting localized tissue repair and gut protection, TB-500 driving systemic cell migration and anti-inflammatory effects. Together they form the most popular recovery stack in peptide research.

Accelerated tissue & tendon repair
Reduced inflammation & swelling
Promotes angiogenesis & blood flow
Synergistic dual-pathway action
Gut & gastrointestinal protection
Stem cell mobilization to injury sites
BPC-157 — View Lab Analysis TB-500 — View Lab Analysis

For Research Purposes Only

BPC-157 Formula

C₆₂H₉₈N₁₆O₂₂

BPC-157 MW

1419.55 g/mol

TB-500 Formula

C₂₁₂H₃₅₀N₅₆O₇₈S

TB-500 MW

4963 g/mol

How the Wolverine Stack Works

BPC-157 and TB-500 operate through distinct but complementary biochemical pathways, making their combination more potent than either compound alone. BPC-157 works locally — targeting the specific site of injury — while TB-500 operates systemically, mobilizing the body's own repair resources from the inside out.

Angiogenesis & VEGF Upregulation

BPC-157 stimulates the formation of new blood vessels by increasing vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) expression — a critical pathway in tissue repair. Enhanced blood flow delivers oxygen and nutrients directly to damaged tissue, significantly accelerating the healing process.

Growth Factor & ERK Pathway Activation

BPC-157 upregulates pro-proliferation pathways including ERK 1/2 phosphorylation and downstream targets c-Fos, c-Jun, and Egr-1 — molecules essential for cell growth, migration, and tissue regeneration. It also increases growth hormone receptor expression in tendon fibroblasts.

Actin Regulation & Cell Migration (TB-500)

TB-500's primary mechanism centers on regulating actin — a protein essential for cell structure and movement. By binding to G-actin and promoting cytoskeletal reorganization, TB-500 enables repair cells to migrate rapidly to injury sites throughout the body.

Stem Cell Mobilization & Anti-Inflammation

TB-500 promotes the mobilization of stem and progenitor cells to form new blood vessels and regenerate tissue. It also blocks neutrophil chemotaxis and reduces macrophage infiltration, decreasing scar formation and fibrosis — complementing BPC-157's localized anti-inflammatory action.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • Tendon & ligament repair
  • Muscle & soft tissue healing
  • Angiogenesis & blood flow
  • Systemic cell migration
  • Reduced scar formation
  • Gastroprotection

Research Note

All findings cited are based on preclinical animal models. Human clinical trials are limited. Neither compound is FDA-approved. Sold strictly for research purposes.

The Two Compounds

BPC-157 and TB-500 each bring a distinct mechanism to the stack. Understanding both reveals why the combination is greater than the sum of its parts.

BPC-157

Body Protection Compound-157

A synthetic pentadecapeptide (15 amino acids) derived from a protective protein naturally found in human gastric juice. First described in 1992, it demonstrates remarkable stability in gastric acid and exhibits cytoprotective properties across multiple organ systems.

  • → Targets localized injury sites directly
  • → Upregulates VEGF & EGR-1 growth factors
  • → Protects gastrointestinal lining
  • → Supports early collagen organization

TB-500

Thymosin Beta-4 Fragment

A synthetic peptide fragment of thymosin beta-4 — a naturally occurring 43-amino acid protein found throughout the human body. It promotes systemic tissue repair by mobilizing cells to injury sites and modulating the inflammatory cascade.

  • → Systemic cell migration via actin regulation
  • → Mobilizes stem & progenitor cells
  • → Reduces scar formation & fibrosis
  • → Cardioprotective properties (preclinical)

"Two pathways. One goal."

Where BPC-157 works locally to repair and protect, TB-500 works systemically to mobilize the body's own regenerative resources — covering both the site of injury and the broader healing cascade simultaneously.

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

The following information is derived from published preclinical research and community-reported protocols. No standardized human dosing has been established. This product is for research purposes only.

BPC-157 Research Protocols

Subcutaneous / IM Injection

250–500 mcg per administration, once or twice daily. Most common protocol in preclinical research models.

Oral Administration

BPC-157 demonstrates unusual stability in gastric acid, allowing for oral routes. Higher amounts typically used due to reduced bioavailability.

Cycle Duration

Typical research cycles: 4–6 weeks, followed by a rest period.

TB-500 Research Protocols

Loading Phase

Conservative protocols: 1.0–1.5 mg subcutaneously or IM, twice weekly (2–3 mg weekly total) for 4–6 weeks.

Maintenance Phase

Following loading: 1–2 mg once weekly to maintain research-observed effects.

Cycling

4–6 weeks active use, 2–4 weeks off before resuming if needed.

Administration Methods

Subcutaneous

Most common. Inject into fatty tissue, often abdomen.

Intramuscular

Near the site of injury for localized application.

Oral (BPC only)

BPC-157 only. Stable in gastric acid.

Reconstitution

Bacteriostatic water. Store at −20°C after mixing.

/ MOTS-C 10 mg

Mitochondria Support
99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 10 MG Mitochondrial Peptide

Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide

MOTS-C

The Mitochondrial Hormone

$50.00 / 10 mg vial

MOTS-C (Mitochondrial Open Reading Frame of the 12S rRNA-c) is a 16-amino acid peptide classified as a Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide — sometimes called a "mitokine" or "mitochondrial hormone." Research indicates it may operate systemically via the bloodstream, influencing metabolic regulation, cellular energy, and longevity pathways from the mitochondria outward.

AMPK pathway activation
Fat metabolism regulation
Skeletal muscle glucose uptake
Insulin sensitivity support
Cellular energy homeostasis
Longevity & anti-aging pathways
View Lab Analysis

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide

Molecular Formula

C₁₀₁H₁₅₂N₂₈O₂₂S₂

Molecular Weight

2174.64 g/mol

Amino Acids

16

How MOTS-C Works

MOTS-C is a 16-amino acid peptide encoded in the mitochondrial genome — specifically the 12S ribosomal RNA region. It is classified as a Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide (MDP), a relatively new category of bioactive molecules that originate in the mitochondria and act as signaling agents throughout the body. Initially researchers believed MOTS-C acted primarily within the mitochondria, but recent studies suggest it enters the bloodstream and exerts systemic effects.

MOTS-C is increasingly described as a "mitokine" — a hormone-like molecule produced by mitochondria that communicates energy status to tissues throughout the body, particularly skeletal muscle.

AMPK Pathway Activation

MOTS-C's most well-studied mechanism involves activation of AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK) — a master regulator of cellular energy homeostasis. AMPK is activated when energy levels are low, spurring glucose and fatty acid uptake. Research suggests MOTS-C acts directly on the methionine folate cycle, increasing AICAR levels and triggering AMPK activation independent of changes in AMP/ATP ratios.

Fat Metabolism & Beta-Oxidation

In animal models, MOTS-C has been shown to regulate monoacylglycerol, dicarboxylate, and sphingolipid metabolism. By reducing activity in these pathways and enhancing beta-oxidation, it may inhibit fat accumulation. Research also suggests it increases brown adipose tissue and reduces dysfunctional adipose accumulation, potentially blocking the inflammation that follows insulin resistance.

Skeletal Muscle & Glucose Uptake

MOTS-C targets skeletal muscle, where it inhibits the folate cycle and the de novo purine biosynthesis pathway, leading to AMPK activation. This enhances skeletal muscle response to glucose uptake — importantly, in a manner independent of the insulin pathway. This offers a potential additional route for muscle cell glucose uptake even when insulin signaling is impaired.

Longevity & Stress Response

MOTS-C levels naturally decline with age, and researchers have noted correlations between MOTS-C levels and age-related metabolic decline. The peptide appears to regulate the cellular response to metabolic stress and may help counteract age-related insulin-resistant muscle changes. Its nuclear activity — particularly in response to stress — is an active area of longevity research.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • AMPK activation
  • Methionine folate cycle
  • Beta-oxidation enhancement
  • Skeletal muscle glucose uptake
  • Insulin sensitivity
  • Brown adipose regulation
  • Mitochondrial signaling

Research Note

MOTS-C research is primarily based on animal models. Human clinical data is emerging. It is not FDA-approved and is sold strictly for research purposes.

Clinical Results

MOTS-C is a newer entrant to research compared to more established peptides, but the body of preclinical evidence is growing rapidly. The following summarizes key findings from published research.

16

Amino acids — encoded directly in the mitochondrial genome

AMPK

Master metabolic regulator activated by MOTS-C in skeletal muscle studies

↓ Age

Natural MOTS-C levels decline with age — linked to metabolic decline in research

Key Findings from Published Research

1

Fat metabolism in obesity models — In laboratory models of obesity, MOTS-C was identified as a potentially vital regulator of monoacylglycerol, dicarboxylate, and sphingolipid metabolism, reducing fat accumulation by enhancing beta-oxidation.

2

Insulin-resistant muscle — Research in mice models indicates MOTS-C may reverse age-related insulin-resistant muscle changes, improving glucose uptake in muscle cells through an insulin-independent pathway.

3

Adipose dysfunction — MOTS-C administration in estrogen-deficient mouse models appeared to increase brown fat prevalence and reduce white adipose accumulation, potentially blocking adipose dysfunction and inflammation following insulin resistance.

4

Longevity correlation — Researchers have noted that MOTS-C has "implications in the regulation of obesity, diabetes, and longevity, representing an entirely novel mitochondrial signaling mechanism to regulate metabolism within and between cells."

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

No standardized human dosing protocols have been established for MOTS-C. The following is derived from preclinical research and community-reported protocols. This product is for research purposes only.

Research Protocol Reference

Typical Research Range

Community protocols commonly reference 5–10 mg per week, administered subcutaneously. Lower starting doses (2–5 mg) are often used initially to assess response.

Administration Route

Subcutaneous injection is the most commonly reported method. Injection into the abdomen or other fatty tissue areas. Some protocols use intramuscular injection near target muscle groups.

Frequency

Once to twice weekly injections are the most commonly reported frequency. Daily lower-dose protocols have also been reported in the research community.

Cycle Length

Typical reported cycles: 4–8 weeks, followed by a rest period of equal or greater length. Given its novel nature, conservative cycling is suggested in research settings.

Reconstitution & Storage

Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store lyophilized powder at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 28 days. Avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles.

99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 10 MG Heptapeptide

Anxiolytic · Nootropic · Immunomodulatory

SELANK

Tuftsin-Derived Anxiolytic

Currently out of stock — restocking soon. Browse the full product details below.

$50.00 / 10 mg vial

Selank (TP-7) is a synthetic heptapeptide developed at the Institute of Molecular Genetics of the Russian Academy of Sciences in the 1990s. An analog of the naturally occurring immune peptide tuftsin, it was engineered with a C-terminal Pro-Gly-Pro extension to dramatically improve metabolic stability. Unlike classical benzodiazepines, Selank modulates multiple neurotransmitter systems simultaneously — delivering anxiolytic and nootropic effects without sedation, cognitive impairment, or dependence.

GABAergic system modulation
BDNF upregulation & neuroplasticity
Enkephalin stabilization
Immune & cytokine modulation
Serotonin system interaction
Anxiolytic without sedation

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

Synthetic Tuftsin Analog

Molecular Formula

C₃₃H₅₇N₁₁O₉

Molecular Weight

751.88 g/mol

Approval Status

Approved in Russia (2009)

How Selank Works

Selank's mechanism is multifaceted, involving at least four distinct neurochemical and immunological pathways. This polypharmacology explains both its broad therapeutic profile and its favorable side effect profile relative to classical anxiolytics. Rather than saturating a single receptor system, Selank modulates several systems at moderate intensity — producing anxiolytic and cognitive effects without the sedation or dependence associated with benzodiazepines.

GABAergic & Serotonergic Modulation

Selank's most extensively studied mechanism involves allosteric modulation of GABA-A receptors, increasing the inhibitory action of GABA — similar in some respects to benzodiazepines, but without the same receptor binding or dependence risk. Research indicates Selank influences the expression of genes involved in GABA signaling and may stimulate gene expression in neurons to affect receptor affinity for GABA. It also interacts with the serotonin system, with studies reporting increases in serotonin metabolite levels and turnover in several brain regions.

BDNF Upregulation & Neuroplasticity

Brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) is a key protein involved in the growth, maintenance, and survival of neurons, as well as synaptic plasticity and learning. Selank has been observed to significantly increase BDNF expression in animal models — an effect not typically seen with classical anxiolytics. This upregulation may contribute to Selank's nootropic and neuroprotective properties, and distinguishes it from benzodiazepines which typically do not enhance cognitive function.

Enkephalin System Stabilization

Enkephalins are naturally occurring opioid peptides with anxiolytic properties that are rapidly degraded by enkephalinase enzymes. Research suggests elevated enkephalinase activity in animal models of anxiety and phobic disorders may deplete these natural anxiolytic peptides. Selank appears to inhibit enkephalinase, stabilizing enkephalins and resetting the enzymatic pathway — preserving the body's own natural anxiolytic mechanisms under stress conditions.

Immune & Cytokine Modulation

Derived from tuftsin — a natural immune peptide — Selank retains immunomodulatory properties. It has been studied for its potential to inhibit IL-6 gene expression in models of depression, and has been shown to affect expression of approximately 34 genes in inflammatory pathways including those affecting chemokines, cytokines, and receptors. Researchers note its adaptogenic properties may be beneficial against environmental stressors.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • GABA-A receptor modulation
  • Serotonin turnover
  • BDNF expression
  • Enkephalin stabilization
  • IL-6 cytokine regulation
  • Stress hormone reduction
  • Immune gene expression (~34 genes)

Research Note

Selank is approved in Russia as a prescription anxiolytic. It is not FDA-approved in the US. Sold strictly for research purposes.

Clinical Results

Selank has undergone more formal clinical investigation than most research peptides, with multiple published trials comparing it directly to established anxiolytic drugs. The following summarizes key findings.

2009

Approved by Russian Ministry of Health for generalized anxiety disorder

4×

Neurotransmitter systems modulated — GABA, serotonin, enkephalin, dopamine

↑ BDNF

Brain-derived neurotrophic factor upregulated — unique vs. classical anxiolytics

Published Clinical Trials

1

Selank vs. Medazepam in GAD — A published clinical trial compared Selank to medazepam (a benzodiazepine) in patients with generalized anxiety disorder. Selank produced comparable anxiolytic effects while showing an improved side effect profile, without the sedation and cognitive blunting associated with medazepam.

2

Selank vs. Phenazepam — A comparison trial against phenazepam found Selank delivered anxiolytic effects with significantly fewer side effects, particularly regarding asthenic symptoms such as fatigue — which Selank was observed to improve rather than worsen.

3

GABAergic Gene Expression Study — Researchers reported Selank influenced expression of 7 genes and moderately affected 45 others, from a set of ~84 genes involved in GABA signaling. The results posited Selank caused a number of alterations in neurotransmission gene expression.

4

Immune & Inflammatory Gene Expression — Selank was found to affect expression of approximately 34 genes in inflammatory pathways. In models of depression, it inhibited the IL-6 gene responsible for the inflammatory cytokine, while control models showed no such effect.

Disclaimer

Clinical trial findings cited above relate to Selank as an investigational and approved compound in Russia, not specifically to this product. This product is for research purposes only.

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

The following is derived from published research and community-reported protocols. No standardized research dosing has been universally established. This product is for research purposes only.

Intranasal (Preferred Route)

Russian Clinical Dosing

The approved Russian formulation is a 0.15% nasal spray. Clinical protocols typically involve 14-day treatment courses with 2–3 drops per nostril, 2–3 times daily.

Research Community Range

250–500 mcg per dose intranasally, 1–3 times daily. Intranasal delivery allows rapid CNS entry within minutes of administration.

Why Intranasal?

The nasal mucosa allows peptides to bypass the blood-brain barrier. Selank crosses into the CNS within minutes via this route, making it the most efficient and widely studied administration method.

Subcutaneous Injection (Alternative)

Typical Range

250–500 mcg subcutaneously, once or twice daily. Some protocols report up to 1 mg per injection in research settings.

Cycle Length

Typical reported cycles: 14–30 days, mirroring Russian clinical protocols. Followed by a rest period before resuming.

Reconstitution & Storage

Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store lyophilized at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 28 days.

Specifications

/ SEMAX 10 mg

99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 10 MG ACTH Analog

Neuroprotective · Nootropic · Cognitive

SEMAX

ACTH-Derived Neuroprotective Peptide

Currently out of stock — restocking soon. Browse the full product details below.

$50.00 / 10 mg vial

Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide derived from the ACTH(4-7) fragment of adrenocorticotropic hormone, fused with a stabilizing Pro-Gly-Pro C-terminal tripeptide. Developed in the early 1980s at the Institute of Molecular Genetics of the Russian Academy of Sciences, it has been approved in Russia for stroke recovery, cognitive disorders, and optic nerve disease. Critically, despite its ACTH origin, Semax is devoid of hormonal activity — it does not stimulate cortisol release, confining its effects entirely to neurotrophic, neuroprotective, and cognitive pathways.

BDNF upregulation
Dopamine & serotonin modulation
Neuroprotection & stroke recovery
Memory & cognitive enhancement
No hormonal activity
Immune & anti-inflammatory effects

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

ACTH(4-7) Synthetic Analog

Molecular Formula

C₃₇H₅₁N₉O₁₀S

Molecular Weight

813.9 g/mol

Approval Status

Approved in Russia (2011)

How Semax Works

Semax acts through multiple converging mechanisms that collectively support neuronal survival, synaptic plasticity, and neurotransmitter balance. Its most prominent feature is the absence of hormonal activity — despite being derived from ACTH, it does not stimulate the adrenal cortex or engage the HPA axis. Its biological activity is confined entirely to neurotrophic, neuroprotective, and immunomodulatory pathways.

BDNF & Neurotrophic Factor Upregulation

Semax's most well-documented mechanism is its potent upregulation of brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) — a key protein governing neuron growth, maintenance, and survival. Research indicates Semax can increase BDNF expression significantly in the CNS, with downstream consequences including enhanced serotonin and dopamine release. This neurotrophic activity forms the foundation of its cognitive and neuroprotective effects, and has been observed in both stroke recovery and cognitive enhancement research.

Dopaminergic & Serotonergic Modulation

Through its BDNF-driven effects and direct receptor interactions, Semax modulates both the dopaminergic and serotonergic neurotransmitter systems. Studies report increases in dopamine and serotonin turnover in several brain regions. These effects contribute to Semax's reported nootropic properties — including improvements in attention, memory consolidation, and cognitive processing speed — without the stimulant profile of amphetamine-class compounds.

Neuroprotective Mechanisms

In ischemia models, Semax appears to promote the expression of 24 genes in the brain and spinal cord through various molecular mechanisms — genes believed to modulate smooth muscle cell migration, red blood cell formation, and angiogenesis. The peptide has been shown to promote neuronal survival and potentially stabilize mitochondria under hypoxic conditions. Researchers characterize its immunomodulating effect on the vascular system during ischemia as a key neuroprotective mechanism.

Immune & Anti-Inflammatory Effects

Semax carries the Pro-Gly-Pro stabilizing tripeptide appended to the ACTH core — a sequence that independently possesses anti-inflammatory properties, being itself a fragment of proline-rich immune-regulatory peptides. This contributes additional immunomodulatory activity beyond the core ACTH fragment's neurotrophic effects, particularly relevant in neuroinflammatory contexts.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • BDNF expression & release
  • Dopamine turnover
  • Serotonin modulation
  • Neuronal survival pathways
  • Angiogenesis & vascular genes
  • Mitochondrial stabilization
  • Immune modulation

Key Distinction

Despite being derived from ACTH, Semax produces zero hormonal activity. It does not stimulate cortisol release or engage the HPA axis — a deliberate design outcome that distinguishes it from its parent hormone.

Research Note

Semax is approved in Russia. It is not FDA-approved in the US. Sold strictly for research purposes.

Clinical Results

Semax has one of the most substantive clinical histories of any research peptide — having been approved and prescribed in Russia since 2011 across multiple indications. The following summarizes key research findings.

2011

Added to Russia's List of Vital & Essential Drugs (ZHVLP)

24

Genes upregulated in brain & spinal cord in ischemia research models

0

Hormonal activity — no cortisol stimulation despite ACTH origin

Research & Clinical Findings

1

Ischemic Stroke Recovery — Semax has been researched extensively in acute cerebral hypoxia models. Studies found that early rehabilitation with Semax increased BDNF plasma levels, accelerated functional recovery, and improved motor performance. The Russian 1% formulation is specifically approved for acute neurological conditions including ischemic stroke.

2

Cognitive Enhancement & Memory — Semax has been studied for cognitive enhancement in healthy subjects and those with memory impairment. Its BDNF upregulation and dopaminergic activity provide a mechanistic basis for improvements in attention, working memory, and cognitive processing. The Russian 0.1% formulation is approved for this indication.

3

Optic Nerve Disease — Published research has examined Semax in glaucomatous neuropathy and other optic nerve conditions. Its neuroprotective and BDNF-stimulating effects appear particularly relevant in retinal ganglion cell preservation contexts. The Russian 1% formulation is approved for this indication.

4

ADHD — Preliminary Research — Early-stage research has examined Semax in the context of attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, with its dopaminergic modulation providing theoretical rationale. Evidence remains preliminary at this stage.

Disclaimer

Clinical findings cited relate to Semax as a pharmaceutical compound approved in Russia, not specifically to this product. This product is for research purposes only and is not FDA-approved.

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

The following is derived from published clinical protocols and research community reports. This product is for research purposes only.

Intranasal (Preferred Route)

Russian Clinical Formulations

0.1% solution for cognitive enhancement and neuroprotection; 1% solution for acute neurological conditions including stroke and optic nerve disease.

Research Community Range

200–600 mcg per dose intranasally, 1–3 times daily. Intranasal delivery allows rapid CNS entry — Semax crosses into the brain within minutes via the olfactory pathway.

Cycle Length

Russian clinical protocols typically use 10–14 day courses. Community protocols often run 2–4 weeks, followed by a rest period.

Subcutaneous Injection (Alternative)

Typical Range

250–500 mcg subcutaneously, once or twice daily. Some research protocols use up to 1 mg per injection.

Variants Available

N-Acetyl Semax (NA-Semax) and N-Acetyl Semax Amidate (NASA) are more potent modified versions with enhanced bioavailability and longer half-life, used at lower doses.

Reconstitution & Storage

Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store lyophilized at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 28 days.

Specifications

/ GHK-Cu 100 mg

Out of Stock
99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 100 MG Copper Tripeptide

Aesthetics · Tissue Repair · Anti-Aging

GHK-Cu

The Copper Tripeptide

Currently out of stock — restocking soon. Browse the full product details below.

$70.00 / 100 mg vial

GHK-Cu (glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine copper complex) is a naturally occurring tripeptide first identified in human plasma in 1973. Found endogenously in blood, saliva, and urine, it plays a central role in tissue remodeling, wound repair, and cellular regeneration. Plasma concentrations decline significantly with age, making it a focus of anti-aging and regenerative research. Computational analysis suggests it may influence the expression of over 4,000 human genes — more than any other single peptide studied.

Collagen & elastin synthesis
Wound healing acceleration
Anti-inflammatory & antioxidant
Hair follicle stimulation
Gene expression modulation
Angiogenesis & copper delivery

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

Copper Tripeptide Complex

Molecular Formula

C₁₄H₂₃CuN₆O₄

First Identified

1973 — Dr. Loren Pickart

Purity

99.5%+

How GHK-Cu Works

GHK-Cu operates through four primary mechanisms that collectively support tissue regeneration, skin repair, and cellular health. What makes GHK-Cu unusual among peptides is that its copper ion isn't merely a structural component — it actively drives enzymatic processes including superoxide dismutase activity and lysyl oxidase-mediated collagen cross-linking, giving it a dual role as both a signaling molecule and a cofactor delivery system.

Collagen & Extracellular Matrix Remodeling

GHK-Cu stimulates synthesis of collagen types I and III, elastin, proteoglycans, and glycosaminoglycans — the core structural proteins of skin and connective tissue. It simultaneously stimulates matrix metalloproteinase (MMP) expression while promoting their inhibitors (TIMPs), a dual action that favors tissue regeneration and structural integrity over simple breakdown. It also increases decorin production, a proteoglycan essential for proper collagen fibril organization, resulting in stronger, better-organized connective tissue.

Anti-Inflammatory & Antioxidant Mechanisms

GHK-Cu demonstrates significant anti-inflammatory properties by modulating cytokine expression — reducing pro-inflammatory markers including TNF-α, IL-6, and TGF-β while promoting anti-inflammatory responses. The copper ion component provides antioxidant benefits as a cofactor for superoxide dismutase (SOD), neutralizing reactive oxygen species (ROS). This reduces oxidative damage and contributes to both wound-healing and anti-aging effects across skin and organs.

Gene Expression Modulation

GHK-Cu can reset gene expression patterns associated with disease and tissue damage toward those more characteristic of healthy tissue. Computational analysis using the Connectivity Map database identified that GHK-Cu may modulate the expression of over 4,000 human genes — upregulating genes involved in stem cell proliferation, DNA repair, and antioxidant responses, while downregulating genes associated with inflammation, tissue destruction, and cancer metastasis. No other peptide studied has been associated with such broad gene expression influence.

Copper Delivery & Angiogenesis

The copper ion serves multiple biological functions beyond antioxidant activity. Copper is essential for angiogenesis — the formation of new blood vessels — which is critical for wound healing and tissue regeneration. GHK-Cu facilitates copper delivery to tissues, supporting lysyl oxidase activity necessary for collagen and elastin cross-linking. Copper also supports nerve regeneration and attracts immune cells and endothelial cells to wound sites, accelerating the complete healing cascade.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • Collagen I & III synthesis
  • Elastin & proteoglycan production
  • MMP / TIMP balance
  • TNF-α & IL-6 reduction
  • Superoxide dismutase (SOD)
  • Gene expression (~4,000 genes)
  • Angiogenesis & copper transport
  • Hair follicle activation

Research Note

GHK-Cu is not FDA-approved for medical use. It is not currently banned by WADA. Sold for research purposes only.

Clinical Results

GHK-Cu has one of the longest research histories of any peptide in this category, having first been identified in 1973. Decades of preclinical and clinical investigation have produced a substantial evidence base across skin, wound healing, and anti-aging applications.

1973

First identified in human plasma by Dr. Loren Pickart — one of the longest-studied peptides

4,000+

Human genes potentially influenced per Connectivity Map computational analysis

↓ Age

Plasma concentrations naturally decline with age — linked to reduced tissue repair capacity

Key Research Findings

1

Wound Healing — Multiple studies have demonstrated GHK-Cu accelerates wound closure, increases tensile strength of healed tissue, and improves the quality of scar formation. It attracts macrophages, mast cells, and capillary-forming cells to wound sites, stimulating the entire healing cascade.

2

Skin Rejuvenation — Clinical research and cosmetic studies have shown GHK-Cu reduces fine lines and wrinkles, increases skin density and firmness, and improves skin clarity. Its stimulation of collagen and elastin synthesis is the primary mechanism, and it is widely used as an active ingredient in cosmetic formulations.

3

Hair Follicle Stimulation — Research indicates GHK-Cu stimulates hair follicle enlargement and prolongs the anagen (growth) phase of the hair cycle. Studies have shown it can increase hair follicle size and improve hair growth density, with topical application being a common route for this application.

4

Gene Expression Reset — Computational analysis using the Connectivity Map database showed GHK-Cu may shift gene expression patterns in diseased cell models — including COPD and cancer — toward healthier profiles. Researchers suggest this broad gene-modulating capacity may underlie many of its diverse effects across different tissue types.

Disclaimer

Research findings cited relate to GHK-Cu as a studied compound, not specifically to this product. The 4,000 gene figure is from computational bioinformatic analysis, not direct experimental measurement. This product is for research purposes only.

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

The following is derived from published research and community-reported protocols. This product is for research purposes only.

Subcutaneous Injection

Typical Research Range

1–2 mg daily via subcutaneous injection. Some protocols use 0.5 mg twice daily. Inject into the fatty tissue of the abdomen or near the target area.

Cycle Length

4–12 weeks is the most commonly reported range. Longer cycles of 8–12 weeks are often used for skin and hair applications where results accumulate over time.

Frequency

Once daily is the most common protocol. Some research subjects use 5 days on / 2 days off to reduce potential tolerance.

Topical Application

Concentration Range

Topical solutions typically use 0.1–2% GHK-Cu concentrations. Can be applied directly to target areas — commonly the face, scalp, or site of injury.

Application Frequency

Once to twice daily topical application. Often used in conjunction with microneedling to enhance skin penetration and collagen stimulation.

Reconstitution & Storage

Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store lyophilized at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 28 days. Protect from light.

/ MOTS-C 10 mg

Mitochondria Support
99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 10 MG Mitochondrial Peptide

Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide

MOTS-C

The Mitochondrial Hormone

$50.00 / 10 mg vial

MOTS-C (Mitochondrial Open Reading Frame of the 12S rRNA-c) is a 16-amino acid peptide classified as a Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide — sometimes called a "mitokine" or "mitochondrial hormone." Research indicates it may operate systemically via the bloodstream, influencing metabolic regulation, cellular energy, and longevity pathways from the mitochondria outward.

AMPK pathway activation
Fat metabolism regulation
Skeletal muscle glucose uptake
Insulin sensitivity support
Cellular energy homeostasis
Longevity & anti-aging pathways
View Lab Analysis

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide

Molecular Formula

C₁₀₁H₁₅₂N₂₈O₂₂S₂

Molecular Weight

2174.64 g/mol

Amino Acids

16

How MOTS-C Works

MOTS-C is a 16-amino acid peptide encoded in the mitochondrial genome — specifically the 12S ribosomal RNA region. It is classified as a Mitochondrial-Derived Peptide (MDP), a relatively new category of bioactive molecules that originate in the mitochondria and act as signaling agents throughout the body. Initially researchers believed MOTS-C acted primarily within the mitochondria, but recent studies suggest it enters the bloodstream and exerts systemic effects.

MOTS-C is increasingly described as a "mitokine" — a hormone-like molecule produced by mitochondria that communicates energy status to tissues throughout the body, particularly skeletal muscle.

AMPK Pathway Activation

MOTS-C's most well-studied mechanism involves activation of AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK) — a master regulator of cellular energy homeostasis. AMPK is activated when energy levels are low, spurring glucose and fatty acid uptake. Research suggests MOTS-C acts directly on the methionine folate cycle, increasing AICAR levels and triggering AMPK activation independent of changes in AMP/ATP ratios.

Fat Metabolism & Beta-Oxidation

In animal models, MOTS-C has been shown to regulate monoacylglycerol, dicarboxylate, and sphingolipid metabolism. By reducing activity in these pathways and enhancing beta-oxidation, it may inhibit fat accumulation. Research also suggests it increases brown adipose tissue and reduces dysfunctional adipose accumulation, potentially blocking the inflammation that follows insulin resistance.

Skeletal Muscle & Glucose Uptake

MOTS-C targets skeletal muscle, where it inhibits the folate cycle and the de novo purine biosynthesis pathway, leading to AMPK activation. This enhances skeletal muscle response to glucose uptake — importantly, in a manner independent of the insulin pathway. This offers a potential additional route for muscle cell glucose uptake even when insulin signaling is impaired.

Longevity & Stress Response

MOTS-C levels naturally decline with age, and researchers have noted correlations between MOTS-C levels and age-related metabolic decline. The peptide appears to regulate the cellular response to metabolic stress and may help counteract age-related insulin-resistant muscle changes. Its nuclear activity — particularly in response to stress — is an active area of longevity research.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • AMPK activation
  • Methionine folate cycle
  • Beta-oxidation enhancement
  • Skeletal muscle glucose uptake
  • Insulin sensitivity
  • Brown adipose regulation
  • Mitochondrial signaling

Research Note

MOTS-C research is primarily based on animal models. Human clinical data is emerging. It is not FDA-approved and is sold strictly for research purposes.

Clinical Results

MOTS-C is a newer entrant to research compared to more established peptides, but the body of preclinical evidence is growing rapidly. The following summarizes key findings from published research.

16

Amino acids — encoded directly in the mitochondrial genome

AMPK

Master metabolic regulator activated by MOTS-C in skeletal muscle studies

↓ Age

Natural MOTS-C levels decline with age — linked to metabolic decline in research

Key Findings from Published Research

1

Fat metabolism in obesity models — In laboratory models of obesity, MOTS-C was identified as a potentially vital regulator of monoacylglycerol, dicarboxylate, and sphingolipid metabolism, reducing fat accumulation by enhancing beta-oxidation.

2

Insulin-resistant muscle — Research in mice models indicates MOTS-C may reverse age-related insulin-resistant muscle changes, improving glucose uptake in muscle cells through an insulin-independent pathway.

3

Adipose dysfunction — MOTS-C administration in estrogen-deficient mouse models appeared to increase brown fat prevalence and reduce white adipose accumulation, potentially blocking adipose dysfunction and inflammation following insulin resistance.

4

Longevity correlation — Researchers have noted that MOTS-C has "implications in the regulation of obesity, diabetes, and longevity, representing an entirely novel mitochondrial signaling mechanism to regulate metabolism within and between cells."

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

No standardized human dosing protocols have been established for MOTS-C. The following is derived from preclinical research and community-reported protocols. This product is for research purposes only.

Research Protocol Reference

Typical Research Range

Community protocols commonly reference 5–10 mg per week, administered subcutaneously. Lower starting doses (2–5 mg) are often used initially to assess response.

Administration Route

Subcutaneous injection is the most commonly reported method. Injection into the abdomen or other fatty tissue areas. Some protocols use intramuscular injection near target muscle groups.

Frequency

Once to twice weekly injections are the most commonly reported frequency. Daily lower-dose protocols have also been reported in the research community.

Cycle Length

Typical reported cycles: 4–8 weeks, followed by a rest period of equal or greater length. Given its novel nature, conservative cycling is suggested in research settings.

Reconstitution & Storage

Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store lyophilized powder at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 28 days. Avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles.

/ SELANK 10 mg

99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 10 MG Heptapeptide

Anxiolytic · Nootropic · Immunomodulatory

SELANK

Tuftsin-Derived Anxiolytic

Currently out of stock — restocking soon. Browse the full product details below.

$50.00 / 10 mg vial

Selank (TP-7) is a synthetic heptapeptide developed at the Institute of Molecular Genetics of the Russian Academy of Sciences in the 1990s. An analog of the naturally occurring immune peptide tuftsin, it was engineered with a C-terminal Pro-Gly-Pro extension to dramatically improve metabolic stability. Unlike classical benzodiazepines, Selank modulates multiple neurotransmitter systems simultaneously — delivering anxiolytic and nootropic effects without sedation, cognitive impairment, or dependence.

GABAergic system modulation
BDNF upregulation & neuroplasticity
Enkephalin stabilization
Immune & cytokine modulation
Serotonin system interaction
Anxiolytic without sedation

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

Synthetic Tuftsin Analog

Molecular Formula

C₃₃H₅₇N₁₁O₉

Molecular Weight

751.88 g/mol

Approval Status

Approved in Russia (2009)

How Selank Works

Selank's mechanism is multifaceted, involving at least four distinct neurochemical and immunological pathways. This polypharmacology explains both its broad therapeutic profile and its favorable side effect profile relative to classical anxiolytics. Rather than saturating a single receptor system, Selank modulates several systems at moderate intensity — producing anxiolytic and cognitive effects without the sedation or dependence associated with benzodiazepines.

GABAergic & Serotonergic Modulation

Selank's most extensively studied mechanism involves allosteric modulation of GABA-A receptors, increasing the inhibitory action of GABA — similar in some respects to benzodiazepines, but without the same receptor binding or dependence risk. Research indicates Selank influences the expression of genes involved in GABA signaling and may stimulate gene expression in neurons to affect receptor affinity for GABA. It also interacts with the serotonin system, with studies reporting increases in serotonin metabolite levels and turnover in several brain regions.

BDNF Upregulation & Neuroplasticity

Brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) is a key protein involved in the growth, maintenance, and survival of neurons, as well as synaptic plasticity and learning. Selank has been observed to significantly increase BDNF expression in animal models — an effect not typically seen with classical anxiolytics. This upregulation may contribute to Selank's nootropic and neuroprotective properties, and distinguishes it from benzodiazepines which typically do not enhance cognitive function.

Enkephalin System Stabilization

Enkephalins are naturally occurring opioid peptides with anxiolytic properties that are rapidly degraded by enkephalinase enzymes. Research suggests elevated enkephalinase activity in animal models of anxiety and phobic disorders may deplete these natural anxiolytic peptides. Selank appears to inhibit enkephalinase, stabilizing enkephalins and resetting the enzymatic pathway — preserving the body's own natural anxiolytic mechanisms under stress conditions.

Immune & Cytokine Modulation

Derived from tuftsin — a natural immune peptide — Selank retains immunomodulatory properties. It has been studied for its potential to inhibit IL-6 gene expression in models of depression, and has been shown to affect expression of approximately 34 genes in inflammatory pathways including those affecting chemokines, cytokines, and receptors. Researchers note its adaptogenic properties may be beneficial against environmental stressors.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • GABA-A receptor modulation
  • Serotonin turnover
  • BDNF expression
  • Enkephalin stabilization
  • IL-6 cytokine regulation
  • Stress hormone reduction
  • Immune gene expression (~34 genes)

Research Note

Selank is approved in Russia as a prescription anxiolytic. It is not FDA-approved in the US. Sold strictly for research purposes.

Clinical Results

Selank has undergone more formal clinical investigation than most research peptides, with multiple published trials comparing it directly to established anxiolytic drugs. The following summarizes key findings.

2009

Approved by Russian Ministry of Health for generalized anxiety disorder

4×

Neurotransmitter systems modulated — GABA, serotonin, enkephalin, dopamine

↑ BDNF

Brain-derived neurotrophic factor upregulated — unique vs. classical anxiolytics

Published Clinical Trials

1

Selank vs. Medazepam in GAD — A published clinical trial compared Selank to medazepam (a benzodiazepine) in patients with generalized anxiety disorder. Selank produced comparable anxiolytic effects while showing an improved side effect profile, without the sedation and cognitive blunting associated with medazepam.

2

Selank vs. Phenazepam — A comparison trial against phenazepam found Selank delivered anxiolytic effects with significantly fewer side effects, particularly regarding asthenic symptoms such as fatigue — which Selank was observed to improve rather than worsen.

3

GABAergic Gene Expression Study — Researchers reported Selank influenced expression of 7 genes and moderately affected 45 others, from a set of ~84 genes involved in GABA signaling. The results posited Selank caused a number of alterations in neurotransmission gene expression.

4

Immune & Inflammatory Gene Expression — Selank was found to affect expression of approximately 34 genes in inflammatory pathways. In models of depression, it inhibited the IL-6 gene responsible for the inflammatory cytokine, while control models showed no such effect.

Disclaimer

Clinical trial findings cited above relate to Selank as an investigational and approved compound in Russia, not specifically to this product. This product is for research purposes only.

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

The following is derived from published research and community-reported protocols. No standardized research dosing has been universally established. This product is for research purposes only.

Intranasal (Preferred Route)

Russian Clinical Dosing

The approved Russian formulation is a 0.15% nasal spray. Clinical protocols typically involve 14-day treatment courses with 2–3 drops per nostril, 2–3 times daily.

Research Community Range

250–500 mcg per dose intranasally, 1–3 times daily. Intranasal delivery allows rapid CNS entry within minutes of administration.

Why Intranasal?

The nasal mucosa allows peptides to bypass the blood-brain barrier. Selank crosses into the CNS within minutes via this route, making it the most efficient and widely studied administration method.

Subcutaneous Injection (Alternative)

Typical Range

250–500 mcg subcutaneously, once or twice daily. Some protocols report up to 1 mg per injection in research settings.

Cycle Length

Typical reported cycles: 14–30 days, mirroring Russian clinical protocols. Followed by a rest period before resuming.

Reconstitution & Storage

Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store lyophilized at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 28 days.

99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 10 MG ACTH Analog

Neuroprotective · Nootropic · Cognitive

SEMAX

ACTH-Derived Neuroprotective Peptide

Currently out of stock — restocking soon. Browse the full product details below.

$50.00 / 10 mg vial

Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide derived from the ACTH(4-7) fragment of adrenocorticotropic hormone, fused with a stabilizing Pro-Gly-Pro C-terminal tripeptide. Developed in the early 1980s at the Institute of Molecular Genetics of the Russian Academy of Sciences, it has been approved in Russia for stroke recovery, cognitive disorders, and optic nerve disease. Critically, despite its ACTH origin, Semax is devoid of hormonal activity — it does not stimulate cortisol release, confining its effects entirely to neurotrophic, neuroprotective, and cognitive pathways.

BDNF upregulation
Dopamine & serotonin modulation
Neuroprotection & stroke recovery
Memory & cognitive enhancement
No hormonal activity
Immune & anti-inflammatory effects

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

ACTH(4-7) Synthetic Analog

Molecular Formula

C₃₇H₅₁N₉O₁₀S

Molecular Weight

813.9 g/mol

Approval Status

Approved in Russia (2011)

How Semax Works

Semax acts through multiple converging mechanisms that collectively support neuronal survival, synaptic plasticity, and neurotransmitter balance. Its most prominent feature is the absence of hormonal activity — despite being derived from ACTH, it does not stimulate the adrenal cortex or engage the HPA axis. Its biological activity is confined entirely to neurotrophic, neuroprotective, and immunomodulatory pathways.

BDNF & Neurotrophic Factor Upregulation

Semax's most well-documented mechanism is its potent upregulation of brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) — a key protein governing neuron growth, maintenance, and survival. Research indicates Semax can increase BDNF expression significantly in the CNS, with downstream consequences including enhanced serotonin and dopamine release. This neurotrophic activity forms the foundation of its cognitive and neuroprotective effects, and has been observed in both stroke recovery and cognitive enhancement research.

Dopaminergic & Serotonergic Modulation

Through its BDNF-driven effects and direct receptor interactions, Semax modulates both the dopaminergic and serotonergic neurotransmitter systems. Studies report increases in dopamine and serotonin turnover in several brain regions. These effects contribute to Semax's reported nootropic properties — including improvements in attention, memory consolidation, and cognitive processing speed — without the stimulant profile of amphetamine-class compounds.

Neuroprotective Mechanisms

In ischemia models, Semax appears to promote the expression of 24 genes in the brain and spinal cord through various molecular mechanisms — genes believed to modulate smooth muscle cell migration, red blood cell formation, and angiogenesis. The peptide has been shown to promote neuronal survival and potentially stabilize mitochondria under hypoxic conditions. Researchers characterize its immunomodulating effect on the vascular system during ischemia as a key neuroprotective mechanism.

Immune & Anti-Inflammatory Effects

Semax carries the Pro-Gly-Pro stabilizing tripeptide appended to the ACTH core — a sequence that independently possesses anti-inflammatory properties, being itself a fragment of proline-rich immune-regulatory peptides. This contributes additional immunomodulatory activity beyond the core ACTH fragment's neurotrophic effects, particularly relevant in neuroinflammatory contexts.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • BDNF expression & release
  • Dopamine turnover
  • Serotonin modulation
  • Neuronal survival pathways
  • Angiogenesis & vascular genes
  • Mitochondrial stabilization
  • Immune modulation

Key Distinction

Despite being derived from ACTH, Semax produces zero hormonal activity. It does not stimulate cortisol release or engage the HPA axis — a deliberate design outcome that distinguishes it from its parent hormone.

Research Note

Semax is approved in Russia. It is not FDA-approved in the US. Sold strictly for research purposes.

Clinical Results

Semax has one of the most substantive clinical histories of any research peptide — having been approved and prescribed in Russia since 2011 across multiple indications. The following summarizes key research findings.

2011

Added to Russia's List of Vital & Essential Drugs (ZHVLP)

24

Genes upregulated in brain & spinal cord in ischemia research models

0

Hormonal activity — no cortisol stimulation despite ACTH origin

Research & Clinical Findings

1

Ischemic Stroke Recovery — Semax has been researched extensively in acute cerebral hypoxia models. Studies found that early rehabilitation with Semax increased BDNF plasma levels, accelerated functional recovery, and improved motor performance. The Russian 1% formulation is specifically approved for acute neurological conditions including ischemic stroke.

2

Cognitive Enhancement & Memory — Semax has been studied for cognitive enhancement in healthy subjects and those with memory impairment. Its BDNF upregulation and dopaminergic activity provide a mechanistic basis for improvements in attention, working memory, and cognitive processing. The Russian 0.1% formulation is approved for this indication.

3

Optic Nerve Disease — Published research has examined Semax in glaucomatous neuropathy and other optic nerve conditions. Its neuroprotective and BDNF-stimulating effects appear particularly relevant in retinal ganglion cell preservation contexts. The Russian 1% formulation is approved for this indication.

4

ADHD — Preliminary Research — Early-stage research has examined Semax in the context of attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, with its dopaminergic modulation providing theoretical rationale. Evidence remains preliminary at this stage.

Disclaimer

Clinical findings cited relate to Semax as a pharmaceutical compound approved in Russia, not specifically to this product. This product is for research purposes only and is not FDA-approved.

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

The following is derived from published clinical protocols and research community reports. This product is for research purposes only.

Intranasal (Preferred Route)

Russian Clinical Formulations

0.1% solution for cognitive enhancement and neuroprotection; 1% solution for acute neurological conditions including stroke and optic nerve disease.

Research Community Range

200–600 mcg per dose intranasally, 1–3 times daily. Intranasal delivery allows rapid CNS entry — Semax crosses into the brain within minutes via the olfactory pathway.

Cycle Length

Russian clinical protocols typically use 10–14 day courses. Community protocols often run 2–4 weeks, followed by a rest period.

Subcutaneous Injection (Alternative)

Typical Range

250–500 mcg subcutaneously, once or twice daily. Some research protocols use up to 1 mg per injection.

Variants Available

N-Acetyl Semax (NA-Semax) and N-Acetyl Semax Amidate (NASA) are more potent modified versions with enhanced bioavailability and longer half-life, used at lower doses.

Reconstitution & Storage

Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store lyophilized at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 28 days.

Specifications

Full OriginACTH(4-7) fragment + Pro-Gly-Pro C-terminal extension
Peptide ClassSynthetic ACTH analog — heptapeptide
Amino Acid SequenceMet-Glu-His-Phe-Pro-Gly-Pro
Molecular FormulaC₃₇H₅₁N₉O₁₀S
Molecular Weight813.9 g/mol
Amino Acids7 (heptapeptide)
Hormonal ActivityNone — no HPA axis engagement
Regulatory StatusApproved in Russia (2011 ZHVLP); not FDA-approved

Purity99.5%+
Storage−20°C (lyophilized) / 2–8°C (reconstituted)

shopping_bag0

/ GHK-Cu 100 mg

Out of Stock
99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 100 MG Copper Tripeptide

Aesthetics · Tissue Repair · Anti-Aging

GHK-Cu

The Copper Tripeptide

Currently out of stock — restocking soon. Browse the full product details below.

$70.00 / 100 mg vial

GHK-Cu (glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine copper complex) is a naturally occurring tripeptide first identified in human plasma in 1973. Found endogenously in blood, saliva, and urine, it plays a central role in tissue remodeling, wound repair, and cellular regeneration. Plasma concentrations decline significantly with age, making it a focus of anti-aging and regenerative research. Computational analysis suggests it may influence the expression of over 4,000 human genes — more than any other single peptide studied.

Collagen & elastin synthesis
Wound healing acceleration
Anti-inflammatory & antioxidant
Hair follicle stimulation
Gene expression modulation
Angiogenesis & copper delivery

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

Copper Tripeptide Complex

Molecular Formula

C₁₄H₂₃CuN₆O₄

First Identified

1973 — Dr. Loren Pickart

Purity

99.5%+

How GHK-Cu Works

GHK-Cu operates through four primary mechanisms that collectively support tissue regeneration, skin repair, and cellular health. What makes GHK-Cu unusual among peptides is that its copper ion isn't merely a structural component — it actively drives enzymatic processes including superoxide dismutase activity and lysyl oxidase-mediated collagen cross-linking, giving it a dual role as both a signaling molecule and a cofactor delivery system.

Collagen & Extracellular Matrix Remodeling

GHK-Cu stimulates synthesis of collagen types I and III, elastin, proteoglycans, and glycosaminoglycans — the core structural proteins of skin and connective tissue. It simultaneously stimulates matrix metalloproteinase (MMP) expression while promoting their inhibitors (TIMPs), a dual action that favors tissue regeneration and structural integrity over simple breakdown. It also increases decorin production, a proteoglycan essential for proper collagen fibril organization, resulting in stronger, better-organized connective tissue.

Anti-Inflammatory & Antioxidant Mechanisms

GHK-Cu demonstrates significant anti-inflammatory properties by modulating cytokine expression — reducing pro-inflammatory markers including TNF-α, IL-6, and TGF-β while promoting anti-inflammatory responses. The copper ion component provides antioxidant benefits as a cofactor for superoxide dismutase (SOD), neutralizing reactive oxygen species (ROS). This reduces oxidative damage and contributes to both wound-healing and anti-aging effects across skin and organs.

Gene Expression Modulation

GHK-Cu can reset gene expression patterns associated with disease and tissue damage toward those more characteristic of healthy tissue. Computational analysis using the Connectivity Map database identified that GHK-Cu may modulate the expression of over 4,000 human genes — upregulating genes involved in stem cell proliferation, DNA repair, and antioxidant responses, while downregulating genes associated with inflammation, tissue destruction, and cancer metastasis. No other peptide studied has been associated with such broad gene expression influence.

Copper Delivery & Angiogenesis

The copper ion serves multiple biological functions beyond antioxidant activity. Copper is essential for angiogenesis — the formation of new blood vessels — which is critical for wound healing and tissue regeneration. GHK-Cu facilitates copper delivery to tissues, supporting lysyl oxidase activity necessary for collagen and elastin cross-linking. Copper also supports nerve regeneration and attracts immune cells and endothelial cells to wound sites, accelerating the complete healing cascade.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • Collagen I & III synthesis
  • Elastin & proteoglycan production
  • MMP / TIMP balance
  • TNF-α & IL-6 reduction
  • Superoxide dismutase (SOD)
  • Gene expression (~4,000 genes)
  • Angiogenesis & copper transport
  • Hair follicle activation

Research Note

GHK-Cu is not FDA-approved for medical use. It is not currently banned by WADA. Sold for research purposes only.

Clinical Results

GHK-Cu has one of the longest research histories of any peptide in this category, having first been identified in 1973. Decades of preclinical and clinical investigation have produced a substantial evidence base across skin, wound healing, and anti-aging applications.

1973

First identified in human plasma by Dr. Loren Pickart — one of the longest-studied peptides

4,000+

Human genes potentially influenced per Connectivity Map computational analysis

↓ Age

Plasma concentrations naturally decline with age — linked to reduced tissue repair capacity

Key Research Findings

1

Wound Healing — Multiple studies have demonstrated GHK-Cu accelerates wound closure, increases tensile strength of healed tissue, and improves the quality of scar formation. It attracts macrophages, mast cells, and capillary-forming cells to wound sites, stimulating the entire healing cascade.

2

Skin Rejuvenation — Clinical research and cosmetic studies have shown GHK-Cu reduces fine lines and wrinkles, increases skin density and firmness, and improves skin clarity. Its stimulation of collagen and elastin synthesis is the primary mechanism, and it is widely used as an active ingredient in cosmetic formulations.

3

Hair Follicle Stimulation — Research indicates GHK-Cu stimulates hair follicle enlargement and prolongs the anagen (growth) phase of the hair cycle. Studies have shown it can increase hair follicle size and improve hair growth density, with topical application being a common route for this application.

4

Gene Expression Reset — Computational analysis using the Connectivity Map database showed GHK-Cu may shift gene expression patterns in diseased cell models — including COPD and cancer — toward healthier profiles. Researchers suggest this broad gene-modulating capacity may underlie many of its diverse effects across different tissue types.

Disclaimer

Research findings cited relate to GHK-Cu as a studied compound, not specifically to this product. The 4,000 gene figure is from computational bioinformatic analysis, not direct experimental measurement. This product is for research purposes only.

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

The following is derived from published research and community-reported protocols. This product is for research purposes only.

Subcutaneous Injection

Typical Research Range

1–2 mg daily via subcutaneous injection. Some protocols use 0.5 mg twice daily. Inject into the fatty tissue of the abdomen or near the target area.

Cycle Length

4–12 weeks is the most commonly reported range. Longer cycles of 8–12 weeks are often used for skin and hair applications where results accumulate over time.

Frequency

Once daily is the most common protocol. Some research subjects use 5 days on / 2 days off to reduce potential tolerance.

Topical Application

Concentration Range

Topical solutions typically use 0.1–2% GHK-Cu concentrations. Can be applied directly to target areas — commonly the face, scalp, or site of injury.

Application Frequency

Once to twice daily topical application. Often used in conjunction with microneedling to enhance skin penetration and collagen stimulation.

Reconstitution & Storage

Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store lyophilized at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 28 days. Protect from light.

Specifications

Molecular FormulaC₁₄H₂₃CuN₆O₄
Amino Acids3 (tripeptide)
First Identified1973, Dr. Loren Pickart
WADA StatusNot currently banned
Amount100 mg
FormLyophilized powder
Purity99.5%+
Storage−20°C (lyophilized) / 2–8°C (reconstituted). Protect from light.

Single Peptide

Retatrutide

Triple Receptor Agonist

Select Dosage

10 mg

$70.00

20 mg

$120.00

Retatrutide is a next-generation peptide that simultaneously activates three receptors — GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon — making it one of the most comprehensively studied compounds for metabolic research. This triple agonist approach provides multi-pathway activity that no single-receptor peptide can match.

GIP receptor activation
GLP-1 receptor activation
Glucagon receptor activation
Multi-pathway metabolic action
Appetite & energy regulation
Fat oxidation support
View Lab Analysis (20 mg batch)

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

Triple Agonist

Receptors

GIP / GLP-1 / Glucagon

Amount

20 mg

Purity

99.5%+

How Retatrutide Works

Retatrutide works by simultaneously activating three distinct incretin and metabolic receptors — GIP (glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide), GLP-1 (glucagon-like peptide-1), and glucagon receptors. This triple agonism approach is what distinguishes Retatrutide from earlier single or dual receptor compounds and provides its unique metabolic research profile.

GLP-1 Receptor Activation

GLP-1 receptor agonism is well-established in metabolic research. Activation of this pathway promotes insulin secretion in a glucose-dependent manner, suppresses glucagon release, slows gastric emptying, and reduces appetite through central nervous system pathways — contributing to energy intake regulation.

GIP Receptor Activation

GIP receptor activation complements GLP-1 action by enhancing insulin secretion from pancreatic beta cells and promoting fat storage regulation. Research suggests the combination of GIP and GLP-1 agonism produces additive or synergistic metabolic effects compared to either alone.

Glucagon Receptor Activation

The glucagon receptor component increases energy expenditure and fat oxidation — mechanisms that single or dual agonists lack. This contributes to the unique metabolic research profile of Retatrutide compared to GLP-1 only compounds, adding a third distinct pathway to its mechanism of action.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • Metabolic regulation
  • Energy expenditure
  • Fat oxidation
  • Appetite modulation
  • Insulin sensitivity
  • Glucose homeostasis

Research Note

Retatrutide is not FDA-approved. Current evidence is primarily from Phase 1/2 clinical trials and preclinical research. Sold strictly for research purposes only.

Clinical Results

Retatrutide has advanced further in clinical development than many research peptides, with Phase 2 trial data published in peer-reviewed literature. The following represents findings from published research — not claims about this product.

~24%

Average body weight reduction in Phase 2 trials at highest doses (48 weeks)

3×

Receptor targets vs. single-agonist compounds — GIP, GLP-1 & Glucagon

Ph.2

Clinical trial stage completed with peer-reviewed published results

Why Triple Agonism Outperforms Dual & Single

1

GLP-1 alone — reduces appetite and slows gastric emptying but lacks the energy expenditure benefits of glucagon activation.

2

GIP + GLP-1 dual — adds insulin sensitization and enhanced fat regulation but still lacks glucagon-driven energy expenditure.

3

GIP + GLP-1 + Glucagon (Retatrutide) — adds direct energy expenditure and fat oxidation via glucagon, covering all three major metabolic pathways simultaneously.

Disclaimer

Clinical results cited above are from published Phase 2 trial data for Retatrutide as an investigational compound, not from this product specifically. This product is for research purposes only.

Dosage Protocol

Important Disclaimer

The following is derived from published clinical trial data and community-reported protocols. No standardized human dosing has been established for research use. This product is for research purposes only.

Research Protocol Reference

Starting Dose

Phase 2 trials used escalating doses starting as low as 0.5 mg weekly via subcutaneous injection, with gradual titration to minimize GI side effects.

Dose Escalation

Doses in Phase 2 trials ranged from 1 mg to 12 mg weekly. Higher doses correlated with greater efficacy but also increased incidence of nausea and other GI effects.

Administration Route

Subcutaneous injection, once weekly. Injection into the abdomen, thigh, or upper arm fatty tissue.

Reconstitution & Storage

Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water. Store lyophilized powder at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 28 days.

Step 2 of 2

Payment

Choose your preferred payment method.

Order Total

$0.00 CAD

Shipping to

—

—

USDC on Solana Network

1 USDC = 1 USD. Send exact order amount.

Wallet Address (Solana)

HEBEQtURNVimpjkLsJDMorqKgUDrStH6XP3dkaZrz5Mi

⚠️ Critical — Read Before Sending

  • • Only send USDC on the Solana network. Do not use Ethereum or any other network.
  • • Send a small test amount first ($5–$10) and confirm receipt before sending the full amount.
  • • You need a small amount of SOL for gas fees (approx. $5 worth).
  • • Crypto transactions cannot be reversed. Double-check the address.

⭐️ How It Works — The Simple Version

💵 What is USDC?

Think of it as a "Digital Dollar." It's a stablecoin — 1 USDC always equals $1 USD. It does not go up and down like Bitcoin.

🚫 No Undo Button

Unlike a bank, there is no customer service. If you send to the wrong address, it is gone forever. Double-check everything.

⛽ "Gas" Money

SOL is the gas you pay to move money. Even with $1,000 in USDC, you need ~$5 of SOL or the transaction won't go through.

🔗 The Chain Rule

Think of a network like a payment system — you can't send from Cash App to Zelle. You must match the network. We use Solana. Always select "Solana" when sending.

⭐️ Option 1: Phantom Wallet (Best for Beginners)

1

Download Phantom Wallet

App Store or phantom.com. It's the "iPhone of crypto wallets" — easiest and safest.

2

Create Your Wallet

Skip the Email/Google login. Choose "Create a Seed Phrase Wallet."

🔑 CRITICAL: Your Seed Phrase

12–24 words. Write them down and hide them. This is the ONLY way to recover your wallet if you lose your phone. NEVER share these words with anyone.

3

Buy SOL (The Gas)

Home page → BUY → Solana (SOL). Use Apple Pay or Debit Card. Buy $5 worth (will last many transactions). Avoid "Bank Transfer" — takes days.

4

Buy USDC

Repeat — BUY → USDC. Buy the order amount plus a little extra for fees. ID verification is normal.

5

Send to Us

Tap Send → USDC. Copy our address above. Send a small test first ($5–$10), confirm it arrived, then send the remainder.

✅

Wait for Confirmation

You'll hear a sound and see a green checkmark. Tap "View Transaction" for your receipt. Then email us at yourpeptideshelf@gmail.com with your order and transaction link.

⭐️ Option 2: Coinbase Way

1

Sign up / Login to Coinbase

Verify your ID. Add cash via Bank or Debit card.

2

Buy SOL + USDC

Buy $5 of SOL (gas — will last many transactions), then buy your USDC amount plus a little extra for fees.

3

Set up Phantom & Transfer

Download Phantom. In Phantom: Receive → Solana → copy your address. In Coinbase: send SOL and USDC to your Phantom address. Select Solana Network.

4

Send to Us from Phantom

Once funds are in Phantom, copy our address above, tap Send → USDC, paste the address, and send!

🇨🇦

Interac E-Transfer

Available for Canadian customers only

Send E-Transfer To

yourpeptideshelf@gmail.com

1

Open Your Banking App

Go to "Send Money" or "Interac E-Transfer" in your Canadian bank app (TD, RBC, Scotiabank, BMO, CIBC, etc.).

2

Enter the Email & Amount

Send to yourpeptideshelf@gmail.com. Enter the exact order total in CAD. In the message/notes field, include your full name and order details.

3

Email Us to Confirm

Email yourpeptideshelf@gmail.com with your name, order, and a screenshot of the transfer confirmation.

Processing Time

E-Transfers are typically received within minutes to a few hours. Your order will be processed once payment is confirmed.

✅

Order Confirmed!

Thank you for your order. Please email yourpeptideshelf@gmail.com with your payment confirmation and order details so we can process your shipment.

What Happens Next

  • 📧 Email us your payment proof
  • 📦 We confirm & prepare your order
  • 🚚 Tracking info sent to your email
