/Melanotan II 10 mg

Out of Stock
99.5%+ Purity Lyophilized 10 MG Cyclic Heptapeptide

Melanocortin · Pigmentation · Aesthetics

Melanotan II

The Tanning Peptide

Currently out of stock — restocking soon.

$50.00/ 10 mg vial

Melanotan II (MT-II) is a synthetic cyclic heptapeptide analogue of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone developed at the University of Arizona in the 1980s. A non-selective agonist at MC1R, MC3R, MC4R, and MC5R — it stimulates melanogenesis through MC1R while producing additional effects on appetite and libido through central receptor activation.

MC1R-mediated melanogenesis
Eumelanin (dark pigment) production
Appetite suppression via MC4R
Libido & sexual function research
Tanning without UV exposure
Predecessor to PT-141 (bremelanotide)

For Research Purposes Only

Peptide Class

Cyclic α-MSH Analogue

Receptors

MC1R, MC3R, MC4R, MC5R

Molecular Formula

C50H69N15O9

Molecular Weight

1024.18 g/mol

How Melanotan II Works

Melanotan II non-selectively binds all four major melanocortin receptors, mimicking alpha-MSH with greater potency and metabolic stability. Its ability to cross the blood-brain barrier allows effects both peripherally in the skin and centrally in the CNS.

MC1R — Melanogenesis & Skin Pigmentation

Activation of MC1R on melanocytes triggers upregulation of eumelanin — the dark pigment responsible for skin darkening. MT-II follows the same biological pathway as UV-induced tanning but without requiring UV exposure. Phase I trials showed measurable tanning after just five doses every other day.

MC4R — Appetite Suppression & Energy Balance

MC4R activation in the CNS modulates energy homeostasis and appetite. Research consistently documents reduced hunger and lower food intake. MC4R is the primary brain pathway regulating body weight and energy expenditure.

MC4R — Sexual Function & Libido

MC4R also mediates spontaneous erections in males — an unexpected finding in early tanning trials that directly led to the development of PT-141 (bremelanotide), now FDA-approved for female sexual arousal disorder. MT-II has demonstrated increased libido in both males and females.

MC3R & MC5R — Immune & Exocrine Effects

MC3R influences inflammation and immune modulation. MC5R is involved in exocrine gland secretion and skin repair — contributing to MT-II's broad physiological profile beyond pigmentation.

Key Mechanisms Affected

  • MC1R — Melanogenesis
  • MC3R — Inflammation / immune
  • MC4R — Appetite & libido
  • MC5R — Exocrine & skin repair
  • Eumelanin synthesis
  • Energy homeostasis

Research Note

Not approved by the FDA, EMA, or any regulatory body. Sold strictly for research purposes. Pre-use dermatological evaluation strongly advised.

Clinical Results

5

Doses for measurable tanning in Phase I trials

4

Melanocortin receptors activated

PT-141

FDA-approved derivative from MT-II research

Key Research Findings

1

Phase I Tanning Trials — Measurable tanning after 5 low-dose injections every other day. Statistically significant increases in skin reflectance and pigmentation scores confirmed MC1R-mediated eumelanin production.

2

Sexual Function Discovery — 8 of 10 male subjects in the landmark trial developed clinically significant erections, directly leading to the development of PT-141 (bremelanotide), now FDA-approved for female hypoactive sexual desire disorder.

3

Appetite Effects — Consistent documentation of appetite suppression through MC4R activation, with reduced hunger and lower food intake.

4

Melanoma Risk Context — A 2013 review found no conclusive evidence MT-II causes melanoma. A 2021 review concluded increased risk is likely from UV-seeking behavior, not direct carcinogenesis. A 2020 in vivo study found MT-II suppressed melanoma progression.

Disclaimer

Findings relate to MT-II as an investigational compound in published research, not specifically to this product. Not approved by any regulatory body. For research purposes only.

Dosage Protocol

Disclaimer

No standardized human dosing exists. The following reflects community protocols and clinical trial data. For research purposes only.

Loading Phase

0.5–1.0 mg subcutaneously every other day for 1–2 weeks. Start low to assess tolerance — nausea is the most common side effect at all dose levels.

Maintenance Phase

Once desired pigmentation is achieved, 0.5–1.0 mg once or twice weekly to maintain results.

Administration

Subcutaneous injection into lower abdomen or lateral thigh using a 27–31 gauge insulin syringe. Injecting in the evening may help manage nausea, which typically peaks 1–2 hours post-dose.

Reconstitution

Add 2 mL bacteriostatic water per 10 mg vial (5 mg/mL solution). Inject water slowly against the vial wall — swirl gently, do not shake.

Storage

Store lyophilized at −20°C. Once reconstituted, refrigerate at 2–8°C and use within 4–6 weeks. Protect from light.

Skin Monitoring Advisory

MT-II can cause reversible darkening of moles and freckles. A baseline dermatological assessment before use and regular skin checks during use are strongly advised.

Specifications

Also Known AsMT-II, MT-2, Melanotan-2
Peptide ClassSynthetic cyclic alpha-MSH analogue
ReceptorsMC1R, MC3R, MC4R, MC5R
Molecular FormulaC50H69N15O9
Molecular Weight1024.18 g/mol
Regulatory StatusNot approved — FDA, EMA, or any jurisdiction
WADA StatusBanned in competitive sport
Amount10 mg
FormLyophilized powder
Purity99.5%+
Storage-20C lyophilized / 2-8C reconstituted (4-6 weeks)
Price$50.00 CAD

For Research Purposes Only. All products are intended strictly for laboratory and scientific research.

flare

About Us

Your Peptide Shelf

Empowering Research Excellence

A Canadian research peptide supplier built on one principle: if we wouldn't use it ourselves, it doesn't go on the shelf.

Our Mission

We built the shelf we wished existed.

The peptide space has a trust problem. Vague sourcing claims, inflated purity numbers, confusing options, and no real transparency. We started Your Peptide Shelf because we were tired of it — as researchers, as consumers, and as people who take this seriously.

Every compound on this shelf is batch-tested in Europe to 99.5%+ purity. Every product page tells you exactly what the research says — not what we want you to believe. And every order ships from Canada with honest pricing, in Canadian dollars, to anywhere in the world.

"Every peptide on this shelf is verified, sourced with integrity, and backed by research. We don't cut corners on purity, we don't hide behind vague claims, and we don't sell compounds we wouldn't use ourselves."

— Your Peptide Shelf

99.5%+

Minimum purity standard on every product

🇪🇺

All batches independently tested in Europe

🇨🇦

Proudly Canadian — shipping worldwide

7

Carefully curated research compounds on the shelf

What We Stand For

Our Values

Verified Purity

99.5%+ purity isn't a marketing claim — it's a hard floor. Every batch is independently tested in Europe before it reaches the shelf. No exceptions. No shortcuts.

Real Transparency

Every product page on this site contains the actual published research — mechanisms, clinical results, dosage protocols, and honest disclaimers. We don't hide behind vague claims.

Integrity First

We only carry compounds we believe in. If a peptide doesn't meet our standards for research evidence, sourcing quality, or purity verification — it doesn't make the shelf, full stop.

Worldwide Access

Research doesn't stop at the border. We ship worldwide from Canada with honest, flat-rate pricing — $20 domestic, $40 international, free on orders over $200 CAD.

Researcher-First

Every decision we make — from pricing to product selection to the depth of our product pages — is made with the researcher in mind. Not the algorithm, not the profit margin.

Evidence-Based

Our product pages are built from peer-reviewed research, published clinical trials, and reputable pharmacological sources. We cite the science, explain the mechanisms, and let the data speak.

The Shelf

Why These Seven Compounds

Every product on this shelf was chosen deliberately — for the depth of its research profile, the quality of evidence behind it, and its relevance to the areas researchers care about most.

BPC

BPC-157 / TB-500 — Wolverine Stack $40

The gold standard recovery stack. BPC-157 and TB-500 are among the most extensively researched regenerative peptides in the literature. Combined, they work through complementary pathways — localized repair and systemic cell migration — making them a cornerstone of recovery research.

RET

Retatrutide 10 mg / 20 mg $70 / $120

The most advanced metabolic peptide currently available for research. As a triple GIP/GLP-1/glucagon receptor agonist, Retatrutide represents the next generation beyond semaglutide and tirzepatide — with clinical data showing unprecedented weight loss outcomes.

MOT

MOTS-C 10 mg $50

A mitochondria-derived peptide with a genuinely unique origin story — it's encoded in mitochondrial DNA, not the nuclear genome. Research links it to improved insulin sensitivity, metabolic regulation, and exercise mimicry, making it one of the most interesting longevity compounds in the field.

SMX

SEMAX 10 mg $50 Out of Stock

A synthetic ACTH analogue approved in Russia for stroke recovery and cognitive enhancement. Semax is unique in being a neuroprotective peptide with no hormonal activity — it doesn't touch the HPA axis despite its ACTH heritage. Strong BDNF upregulation and clinical trial history.

SLK

SELANK 10 mg $50 Out of Stock

The anxiolytic companion to Semax. Selank is a tuftsin-derived heptapeptide approved in Russia for generalized anxiety disorder — delivering benzodiazepine-comparable anxiolytic effects through GABA modulation, without dependence, sedation, or cognitive blunting.

GHK

GHK-Cu 100 mg $70 Out of Stock

The copper tripeptide that has been studied since 1973. GHK-Cu is the most gene-expression-broad compound in peptide research — computational analysis suggests it influences over 4,000 human genes. Collagen synthesis, wound healing, hair follicle stimulation, and anti-inflammatory action.

MT2

Melanotan II 10 mg $50 Out of Stock

One of the most researched melanocortin peptides in existence. Developed at the University of Arizona in the 1980s, MT-II's early tanning trials led directly to the discovery of PT-141 (bremelanotide), now FDA-approved for sexual arousal disorder. It activates four melanocortin receptors, producing effects on skin pigmentation, appetite, and libido — making it a compound of interest across multiple research domains.

/ Bacteriostatic Water 30 mL

In Stock
30 mL Vial Multi-Dose Pfizer / Hospira USP Standard

Reconstitution · Diluent · Multi-Dose

Bacteriostatic Water

Pfizer Hospira — 30 mL USP

$15.00 / 30 mL vial

Pfizer's Hospira Bacteriostatic Water for Injection, USP is the industry benchmark for peptide reconstitution. Every lyophilized peptide on this shelf requires a reliable diluent before use — this is it. A sterile, non-pyrogenic multi-dose vial preserved with 0.9% benzyl alcohol, manufactured under Pfizer's strict quality assurance protocols and meeting full USP standards.

Why You Need This

All peptides on this shelf ship as lyophilized (freeze-dried) powder. Before use in any research protocol, they must be reconstituted with a sterile aqueous vehicle. Bacteriostatic water is the standard of choice — the benzyl alcohol preservative allows repeated withdrawals over 28 days without compromising sterility, unlike plain sterile water which is single-use only.

Multi-dose — up to 28 days after first use
0.9% benzyl alcohol bacteriostatic preservative
Pfizer Hospira — USP standard
Non-pyrogenic, sterile, pH 4.5–7.0
Compatible with all peptides on this shelf
30 mL — enough for multiple reconstitutions

For Research Purposes Only

Manufacturer

Pfizer / Hospira

Volume

30 mL Multidose

Preservative

0.9% Benzyl Alcohol

Standard

USP / Sterile / Non-Pyrogenic

Why Bacteriostatic Water

Every peptide on this shelf ships as lyophilized powder — freeze-dried for stability during storage and shipping. Before any research protocol, that powder must be dissolved into a stable aqueous solution. The diluent you choose directly impacts sterility, peptide stability, and the reliability of your results across multiple draws. Bacteriostatic water is the research standard for good reason.

Benzyl Alcohol — The Key Difference

Plain sterile water is single-use only — once the vial is punctured, any subsequent withdrawal risks bacterial contamination. Bacteriostatic water contains 0.9% benzyl alcohol (9 mg/mL), which inhibits bacterial reproduction without killing bacteria outright (bacteriostatic, not bactericidal). This preservative action maintains sterility through repeated needle withdrawals for up to 28 days — essential for multi-dose peptide research protocols.

Peptide Stability & Integrity

Peptides are fragile molecules that can be degraded by bacterial byproducts, heat, light, and contamination. Using the wrong solvent — or a compromised one — can cause foaming (risking oxidation), precipitation (insoluble aggregates), or rapid degradation. Bacteriostatic water provides a chemically neutral, sterile environment that preserves peptide structure and biological activity, keeping reconstituted solutions potent and consistent across the full 28-day period.

Why Pfizer Hospira

Hospira, a Pfizer subsidiary, is widely regarded as the gold standard manufacturer for injectable-grade aqueous solutions. Their bacteriostatic water is produced under strict pharmaceutical Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) guidelines, with a semi-rigid polyolefin vial confirmed safe per USP biological standards for plastic containers. It's the formulation referenced in most peptide reconstitution guides for a reason — reliability and consistency batch after batch.

BAC Water vs Alternatives

✅ Bacteriostatic Water

Multi-dose, 28-day stability, benzyl alcohol preservative. The standard for all peptides on this shelf.

⚠️ Sterile Water

Single-use only. No preservative — bacterial contamination risk within hours of opening. Unsuitable for multi-dose protocols.

⚠️ Saline (0.9% NaCl)

Introduces ionic variables that can shift stability. May cause aggregation in hydrophobic peptide sequences. Not recommended.

28-Day Rule

Once the vial is first punctured, use within 28 days and refrigerate at 2–8°C after opening. Label with date of first use. Discard after 28 days even if solution remains.

How to Reconstitute Your Peptides

Important

Always use aseptic technique. Work on a clean surface, wear gloves, and use a fresh sterile syringe for every withdrawal. For research purposes only.

1

Calculate Your Volume

Decide on your target concentration. For a 10 mg vial, adding 2 mL of bacteriostatic water yields a 5 mg/mL solution — easy dosing math. Use a peptide calculator if unsure. The exact volume determines your concentration, so measure carefully.

2

Draw the Bacteriostatic Water

Wipe the rubber stopper of the BAC water vial with an alcohol swab. Using a fresh sterile syringe, draw the exact volume needed. Avoid touching the needle to any surface.

3

Inject Gently Into Peptide Vial

Wipe the peptide vial stopper with an alcohol swab. Insert the needle and angle it so the water runs slowly down the inner wall of the vial — never blast it directly onto the powder. This prevents foaming and protein denaturation.

4

Dissolve — Swirl, Never Shake

Gently swirl the vial in slow circular motions. Most peptides dissolve within 1–5 minutes. Never shake vigorously — this causes foaming and risks oxidation. If needed, gently warm the vial in your palm. The solution should be clear when fully dissolved.

5

Label, Inspect & Store

Confirm the solution is clear with no unexpected precipitation or discolouration. Label the vial with the peptide name, concentration, and date of reconstitution. Refrigerate at 2–8°C immediately. Use fresh needles for every draw.

Common Concentrations Reference

Peptide VialBAC Water AddedConcentration
10 mg2 mL5 mg/mL
10 mg1 mL10 mg/mL
20 mg2 mL10 mg/mL
100 mg10 mL10 mg/mL

Why BD Insulin Syringes

BD (Becton Dickinson) has been the global standard for insulin syringes for decades. Their Ultra-Fine needle technology is designed for subcutaneous injection — the same route used for most peptide research protocols. The 30 gauge, 0.30 mm needle is fine enough to minimize discomfort and tissue trauma, while the 1 mL barrel provides the precision needed to measure small peptide doses accurately.

Why 30 Gauge for Peptides

Gauge refers to needle thickness — the higher the number, the finer the needle. At 30G, the needle is fine enough for minimal tissue disruption and a nearly painless injection while still allowing smooth flow of aqueous peptide solutions. Most peptide research protocols specify 27–31G insulin syringes for subcutaneous injection. 30G sits in the ideal middle ground.

Why 1 mL Capacity

A 1 mL insulin syringe allows measurement in IU (international units) or mL. For a typical peptide reconstituted at 5 mg/mL in 2 mL of bacteriostatic water, a 1 mL syringe gives you the range and precision to draw exact doses — whether 0.1 mL (500 mcg) or 0.5 mL (2.5 mg). The graduated markings make dosing straightforward and repeatable.

Alcohol Prep Pads Included

Each kit includes 10 individually wrapped 70% isopropyl alcohol prep pads — one per injection. Alcohol swabs are used to sterilize the rubber stoppers of both the BAC water vial and the peptide vial before each needle insertion, and to clean the injection site before administration. Using a fresh swab every time is an essential part of aseptic technique.

What's in the Kit

  • 10 × BD 1 mL insulin syringes
  • 30G × 0.30 mm attached needle
  • Orange safety cap per syringe
  • 10 × alcohol prep pads (70% IPA)
  • Individually sterile-wrapped
  • Single-use, latex-free

Bundle & Save

Order BAC Water + Syringes Kit together for a complete injection setup. One 30 mL BAC water vial paired with one 10-pack syringe kit covers reconstitution and injection for multiple peptide vials.

Subcutaneous Injection Guide

Disclaimer

The following reflects standard aseptic technique for research use with subcutaneous injection. For research purposes only.

1

Prepare Your Workspace

Wash hands thoroughly. Put on clean gloves. Work on a clean, flat surface. Have your reconstituted peptide vial, BAC water, fresh syringe, and alcohol swabs ready.

2

Swab the Vial Stopper

Wipe the rubber stopper of your peptide vial with an alcohol prep pad using a firm circular motion. Allow 10–15 seconds to air dry fully before inserting the needle.

3

Draw Your Dose

Remove the orange cap from the syringe. Insert the needle through the stopper and invert the vial. Pull the plunger back to draw your desired volume. Remove air bubbles by gently tapping the barrel and pushing them out.

4

Swab the Injection Site

Select a subcutaneous site — lower abdomen, lateral thigh, or outer upper arm. Wipe with a fresh alcohol swab and allow to dry. Pinch a fold of skin gently between two fingers.

5

Inject at 45°

Insert the needle at a 45° angle into the pinched skin fold with a smooth, confident motion. Release the skin fold. Slowly depress the plunger at a steady pace. The 30G needle should produce minimal sensation.

6

Withdraw & Dispose

Withdraw the needle at the same angle it entered. Apply gentle pressure with a clean swab — do not rub. Recap the needle and dispose of the entire syringe in a sharps container. Never reuse syringes.

Dose Conversion Reference (1 mL Syringe)

Concentration250 mcg500 mcg1 mg
2.5 mg/mL0.10 mL0.20 mL0.40 mL
5 mg/mL0.05 mL0.10 mL0.20 mL
10 mg/mL0.025 mL0.05 mL0.10 mL
